name: Tepoztlán

palette.name: Tepozteco Green
palette.rationale: Olive green represents the protected mountain vegetation surrounding the town and Tepozteco ridge.

palette.name: Cliff Ochre
palette.rationale: Warm ochre translates the rocky hillside and archaeological setting into a usable earth tone.

palette.name: Convent Wall
palette.rationale: Light stone and plaster reference the Ex Convento de Nuestra Señora de la Natividad as a neutral layer.

palette.name: Chinelo Orange
palette.rationale: A bright orange accent nods to carnival costume energy and local festival color without fixing a single costume palette.

palette.name: Mountain Dusk
palette.rationale: Deep blue-green adds a dusk and shadow anchor for mountain views and evening plaza use.

sources.title: Tepoztlán, Morelos
sources.publisher: Secretaría de Turismo, Gobierno de México
sources.note: Used for Pueblo Mágico, protected natural setting, convent, and Tepozteco context.

sources.title: Zona Arqueológica Tepozteco
sources.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Antropología e Historia
sources.note: Used for the archaeological site and mountain sanctuary context.

notes: Standard starter record; colors are editorial readings of landscape, convent architecture, archaeology, and festival atmosphere.
editorialSummary: Tepoztlán's starter palette builds around the Tepozteco landscape, with green vegetation, ochre cliff, pale convent stone, a festival orange accent, and mountain dusk as a practical anchor.
